safest_plan.entry_basis: zero_live (5 Jun) — mid of the IVB re-accumulation/entry zone drawn on the chart ($76–$80); the trade is already triggered, so this is the zone to wait for on a pullback, not a fresh signal at current price (~$88).

targets.basis: zero_live chart — low-volume node above / 'Remains TP, partial' zone (~$96), the next stated objective.
zero_live chart — 'Second potential TP' line (chart only; not in extracted text).

safest_plan.stop_basis: Below the 0.618 fib at 74.14 and the base of the $76–$80 entry zone (the structure the re-accumulation sits on). Zero's own guidance is a break-even stop below the point of control.

stance_by_source.summary: Trade already up ~24% from the re-accumulation zone and working; take partial profit and move stop to break-even, aim for the low-volume node above — do not chase.

conflicts: Chart vs text: the image shows two further upside targets ($107 'Second potential TP' and $117 'Third potential TP') and a structural floor at the 0.618 fib (74.14) that the extracted text omitted — these are added from the chart.

The single source frames this as an already-triggered trade to manage ('do not chase'), so there is no fresh entry for a new buyer at the current ~$88 price; the clean $76–$80 zone is below the market.
plain_english: Only one source weighed in — Zero's 5 June live session — and it was bullish, but it described a trade that was already up about 24% and working. Its advice was to take some profit and move the stop to break-even, not to buy more. The clean buy zone it drew sits lower at roughly $76–$80, and the trade only fails if price falls back below about $74. With price already near $88, buying now means chasing a move that has already run, so the sensible play is to wait for a pullback into the $76–$80 zone rather than enter here.
